items.title: Microsofts Record 622-CVE July 2026 Patch Tuesday Ships an Actively Exploited SharePoint Zero-Day
items.description: Microsofts July 2026 Patch Tuesday is the largest in the companys history, addressing 622 CVEs across Windows, Office, Azure, SharePoint, Exchange, and more. Buried inside that record volume is the fix that should jump to the top of every cloud security teams list: CVE-2026-56164, an unauthenticated, actively exploited zero-day in SharePoint Server. If you run []
items.apex_ai_summary: [CRITICAL] THREAT-INTEL advisory. Risk score 8.5/10. Full APEX AI analysis available to Pro subscribers.
items.ioc_note: Actor-attribution campaign — IOC set derived from threat actor research, not automated feed extraction.

apex.ai_summary: [P1] CVE / Vulnerability - critical severity (Risk 9.0/10). No MITRE ATT&CK techniques mapped - limited adversarial context. 1 indicator detected - upgrade to Pro for full IOC access. Actor attribution: CDB-CVE-GEN. AI confidence: LOW (22%) - limited source data available.
apex.recommended_action: CRITICAL PRIORITY - Patch within 24-48 hours. Activate IR playbook. Notify SOC lead and CISO.

ioc_paywall.message: 1 IOC(s) at 7.0% confidence - unlock with Pro tier.

items.kev_name: Microsoft SharePoint Server Missing Authentication for Critical Function Vulnerability
items.kev_action: Apply mitigations in accordance with vendor instructions, ensuring compliance with CISA’s BOD 26-04 Prioritizing Security Updates Based on Risk (see URL in Notes) guidance and CISA’s “Forensics Triage Requirements” (see URL in Notes). Follow applicable BOD 26-04 guidance for cloud services or discontinue use of the product if mitigations are unavailable. Stakeholders are responsible for evaluating each asset's internet exposure and ensuring adherence to BOD 26-04 patching guidelines.
items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Microsofts Record 622-CVE July 2026 Patch Tuesday Ships an Actively Exploited Sh
id: c2c3cb8e-b845-5d5d-8f68-072deb0f4707
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-56164 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: KEV_EXPLOIT
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-56164
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: critical
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-56164 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-16T06:37:09Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-56164", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-56164 Exploitation Attempt - KEV_EXPLOIT"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:5949568; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Intelligence confirmed: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities; Active exploitation language detected in intelligence text; EPSS score 7.050 (very high exploitation probability)

confidence_factors.signal: Item is listed in CISA Known Exploited Vulnerabilities
Active exploitation language detected in intelligence text

items.recommended_sla_action: EMERGENCY PATCH — Active exploitation confirmed or KEV listed
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-15694: A vulnerability was detected in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. Impacted is the function fromSetIpBind of the file /goform/S
items.description: A vulnerability was detected in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. Impacted is the function fromSetIpBind of the file /goform/SetIpBind. The manipulation of the argument page results in stack-based buffer overflow. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit is now public and may be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-15696: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. The impacted element is the function fromVirtualSer of the
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. The impacted element is the function fromVirtualSer of the file /goform/VirtualSer. Such manipulation of the argument page leads to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-15695: A flaw has been found in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. The affected element is the function fromDhcpListClient of the file
items.description: A flaw has been found in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. The affected element is the function fromDhcpListClient of the file /goform/DhcpListClient. This manipulation of the argument page causes stack-based buffer overflow. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.

items.title: CVE-2026-15691: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. This affects the function fromSafeClientFilter of the
items.description: A security flaw has been discovered in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. This affects the function fromSafeClientFilter of the file /goform/SafeClientFilter. Performing a manipulation of the argument page results in stack-based buffer overflow. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-15692: A weakness has been identified in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. This vulnerability affects the function fromSafeUrlFilter
items.description: A weakness has been identified in Tenda BE12 Pro 16.03.66.23. This vulnerability affects the function fromSafeUrlFilter of the file /goform/SafeUrlFilter. Executing a manipulation of the argument page can lead to stack-based buffer overflow. The attack may be performed from remote. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks.

items.title:
CVE-2026-49488: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability in Apache OpenMeetings.

Th
items.description:
Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal') vulnerability in Apache OpenMeetings.

This issue affects Apache OpenMeetings: from 5.0.0 before 9.1.0.
An attacker with moderator rights in any room can read arbitrary files accessible to the OS account running the OM server, including credentials and secrets, via a crafted download request.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 9.1.0, which fixes the issue.

items.sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-49488 Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory Path 
id: 0672cd4d-8037-526d-8e4d-6a00c5949cf8
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-49488 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: PATHTRAVERSAL
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-49488
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
items.kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-49488 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-16T06:37:09Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-49488", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
items.suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-49488 Exploitation Attempt - PATHTRAVERSAL"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7377644; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-16, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
items.confidence_rationale: Medium confidence — EPSS score 0.733 (very high exploitation probability)
items.recommended_sla_action: SCHEDULED PATCH — Apply in next maintenance window
items._tier_notice: Upgrade to PRO for full enrichment: actor attribution, kill chain, IOC hashes, AI analysis, STIX bundle.

items.title: CVE-2026-62390: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection') vulnerability in Apache Kylin. A ba
items.description:
Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command ('SQL Injection') vulnerability in Apache Kylin. A backend API refreshing table catalog may cause the injection to the generated SQL.

This issue affects Apache Kylin: from 4 through 5.0.3.

Users are recommended to upgrade to version 5.0.4, which fixes the issue.

id: 3d2f4727-3a61-59e8-aa31-e04560c3399c
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-62390 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: HIGH | Class: SQLI
references:

author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/16
modified: 2026/07/16
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: webserver
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        cs-uri-query|contains:
            - "' OR 1=1"
            - "UNION SELECT"
            - "'; DROP TABLE"
            - "1=1--"
            - "benchmark("
            - "sleep("
            - "0x"
        sc-status|contains: '500'
    filter_legitimate:
        cs-uri-stem|startswith: '/api/search'
    condition: selection and not filter_legitimate
falsepositives:
    - Web application firewall test traffic
    - Vulnerability scanner activity
level: high

items.title: CVE-2026-15669: A vulnerability was found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This issue affects the function ExecTool.Execute of the file
items.description: A vulnerability was found in louisho5 picobot up to 0.2.0. This issue affects the function ExecTool.Execute of the file internal/agent/tools/exec.go of the component exec Tool. The manipulation results in os command injection. The attack requires a local approach. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but has not responded yet.
